segments.text: On that night could not the king sleep, and he commanded to bring the book of records of the chronicles; and they were read before the king.

footnotes: could not: Heb. the king's sleep fled away

segments.text: And it was found written, that Mordecai had told of Bigthana and Teresh, two of the king's chamberlains, the keepers of the door, who sought to lay hand on the king Ahasuerus.
And the king said,
What honour and dignity hath been done to Mordecai for this?
segments.speaker: King Ahasuerus

segments.text: Then said the king's servants that ministered unto him,
There is nothing done for him.
segments.speaker: King's Servants

segments.text: And the king said, Who is in the court? Now Haman was come into the outward court of the king's house, to speak unto the king to hang Mordecai on the gallows that he had prepared for him.
And the king's servants said unto him,
Behold, Haman standeth in the court.
segments.speaker: King's Servants

segments.text: And the king said,
Let him come in.
segments.speaker: King Ahasuerus

segments.text: So Haman came in. And the king said unto him,
What shall be done unto the man whom the king delighteth to honour?
segments.speaker: King Ahasuerus

segments.text: Now Haman thought in his heart,
To whom would the king delight to do honour more than to myself?
segments.speaker: Haman

footnotes: whom the king delighteth to honour: Heb. in whose honour the king delighteth

segments.text: And Haman answered the king,
For the man whom the king delighteth to honour,
segments.speaker: Haman

footnotes: whom the king delighteth to honour: Heb. in whose honour the king delighteth

segments.text: Let the royal apparel be brought which the king useth to wear, and the horse that the king rideth upon, and the crown royal which is set upon his head:
segments.speaker: Haman

footnotes: Let them bring the royal apparel

segments.text: And let this apparel and horse be delivered to the hand of one of the king's most noble princes, that they may array the man withal whom the king delighteth to honour, and bring him on horseback through the street of the city, and proclaim before him, Thus shall it be done to the man whom the king delighteth to honour.
segments.speaker: Haman

segments.text: Then the king said to Haman,
Make haste, and take the apparel and the horse, as thou hast said, and do even so to Mordecai the Jew, that sitteth at the king's gate: let nothing fail of all that thou hast spoken.
segments.speaker: King Ahasuerus

footnotes: let nothing fail: Heb. suffer not a whit to fall

segments.text: Then took Haman the apparel and the horse, and arrayed Mordecai, and brought him on horseback through the street of the city, and proclaimed before him, Thus shall it be done unto the man whom the king delighteth to honour.
And Mordecai came again to the king's gate. But Haman hasted to his house mourning, and having his head covered.
And Haman told Zeresh his wife and all his friends every thing that had befallen him.
Then said his wise men and Zeresh his wife unto him,
If Mordecai be of the seed of the Jews, before whom thou hast begun to fall, thou shalt not prevail against him, but shalt surely fall before him.
segments.speaker: Zeresh and Wise Men

segments.text: And while they were yet talking with him, came the king's chamberlains, and hasted to bring Haman unto the banquet that Esther had prepared.
